summary: Reviewer R10 completed a read-only review of the unfinished DinoBrain OS completion plan against the governing completion conditions, verification docs, AGENTS protocol, verify:goal implementation, memory registry, and current state artifacts. Verdict: AGREE; no blocking objections. Evidence showed the plan does not claim completion, requires verify:goal/status:health and missing verifier work, blocks on semantic RAG and answer-quality evidence, tracks task lifecycle blockers, and keeps consensus pending until ten AGREE results in one round.
decisions: AGREE: The final completion bar is sufficient because it requires real verify:goal/status:health, direct MCP proof, native authority scanning, source lineage, behavior recall, semantic RAG plus answer-quality, Observatory parity, public-data safety, and ten-reviewer final agreement.

No blocking objection: The plan explicitly avoids false completion by stating it is not complete, keeping final consensus pending, distinguishing scaffold RAG from completion-grade RAG, and requiring verify:goal to fail when semantic provider or answer-quality evidence is missing.

Current artifacts corroborate non-completion rather than false-green: health_status is needs_attention due to task lifecycle blockers, rag_proof uses local_text_hashing_v1 with semantic_embedding_provider false, and rag_eval caveats call the report a deterministic canary.
next_steps: Proceed with remaining implementation packages in the documented order; rerun all ten final reviewers only after any future document change.
